submission.title: Hello, I just discovered term ‘derealization’
submission.body:
I have this feeling of fog, and surrounding seems muted, but I thought everyone had this feeling. I have it since I was a kid, and it usually lasts for several seconds as I can stop it, and I like it for 5-10 seconds and it calms me. Is it the same thing? Should it be treated if I like it? 
I noticed that I didn’t have it for a long time, while I am living alone, it usually happens when I am living with other people

comments.body:
It's related to anxiety, usually it gets triggered when you are anxious. 

I've had it my whole life like you, I would get brief 5 seconds of detachment occasionally. Just 5 years ago it have become a disorder for me since I'm experiencing it 24/7 for 5 years.
Oh it is bad, I have anxiety, and I had panic attacks during my childhood, but now it is much better. Thank you a lot for sharing this

submission.title: Floating like a balloon and can't reach ground
submission.body:
I keep having the same dream, I swear, almost every night--maybe every other night at best. The setting of the dream can take place in any other dream, this has occurred in every type of dream I've ever had, so it's more like a secondary event that happens on top of the already occurring dream I have. Things will be going completely normally, then all of a sudden, I will simply start floating like a balloon higher and higher into the sky until I'm about 20ft maybe 25 ft in the air. Whoever I am talking to in the dream, which is usually what I'm doing in my dreams, will simply not react and keep speaking to me like nothing is happening (even yelling at me if I've floated very far up so that I can hear them). However, once I've floated up high, I spend the entire rest of the dream focusing on getting back down. I have to put all of my effort into trying to float back down, I can't even describe to you the kind of feeling I have to create but (it's like moving a muscle that I don't have in real life) and I have to really use it or I will never get back down. I usually don't get back down. I will sometimes float even higher if I start to get tired of trying to get back down, and I've reached all the way into the sky where there are literally only clouds and nothing else. There is 1 trick I've found to getting down, and I basically have to accept that I have to fall to the ground, and so once I do I will start falling. It then turns into your classic falling dream. I've figured out that if I close my eyes right before I hit the ground then it's not as scary, but I will still jolt myself awake in real life once I hit the ground (even if my eyes are closed and I don't know WHEN i'm going to hit the ground, my mind does I guess).   
What the hell could this possibly mean and why does it keep happening?!

comments.body:
i suppose it's a boredom dream, trying to have new ideas, not really sure how to put all the mental floaty stuff into practice. maybe you feel like you end up having communication issues, people aren't on the same wavelength. if you get too high it ends up 'cloudy' you probably lose track of where you are. some people describe themselves as 'dreamy' or 'head in the clouds'. 

and there's a bit of a trauma aspect to falling. negative trauma associations with reality and practical issues. even if you came up with something clever, it might seem stressful and a lot of work to file paperwork and patent it.

i imagine you could become more aware of things that upset you with reality, stressful tasks, difficult communications, and develop that type of muscle.

I've been experiencing this too! I started having this kind of dream just this year. These past few weeks it has been reoccurring, I even had it for 3 consecutive nights but they were on different settings. Whenever I float, I also have the urge to go back down. People around me stare but they don't think that anything seems odd. Just like yours, they still continue to interact with me normally. I also feel that my legs are in a very weak state, very much like the post needles-and-pin phase when you slept on your arm. I feel it very vividly, and I sometimes even feel it when I wake up. I also remember feeling very uncomfortable in each of the dreams I had where I'm floating uncontrollably.

Unlike you, I still haven't found a trick to control going down. Either I give up and keep myself afloat or cling onto something heavy. I feel like the character Emma from Ms. Peregrine's Home for Peculiar Children (I haven't read or watched it). I also haven't reach the point where I'm super way up high. My highest is probably 20-stories high. 

I really don't know how to interpret this, but I'm glad to have found someone who experiences somewhat similar.
You are awesome at what you do. This is pretty spot on, and I'm dead serious about that.

Yes, every time I float away, it is not a pleasant feeling what-so-ever. I hate it. It's not fun. I want to get back to the ground. I have a few different tricks to getting back down, because I've been having this dream pretty much every night for 6 months or so now. I've been able to make it down maybe 7 times in total, and it takes a great deal of mental effort. I will be straining my 'flying muscle' for what feels like eternity until I finally start to slowly come back down to the ground. If I've made it the ground, I have a feeling that I'm going to float away if I don't constantly focus on staying on the ground so I still have to concentrate (sorta) on my 'flying muscle' from that point, but at least I made it. The other way, is like I mentioned, somehow I 'accept' that falling is the only solution (and I am only able to do this after I reach a sufficient height, If I can still see the ground this isn't a possibility). However, if I've managed to float to the clouds, I can accept that I have to fall and it will happen. This is basically accepting that I have to end the dream too.

Personally, I think with how much control I have during these floating portions of my dreams, I think it's a semi-lucid state--or more like a failed-lucid state. I have had successful lucid dreams happen, and all the sudden, I always think 'wow this is just like real life holy shit' and I start running and seeing my surroundings as quickly as I can because I know my heart rate in real life always increases and wakes me up so I can't enjoy more than 10 sec or so of an actual lucid dream. I think the floating happens, when I was almost going to start a true lucid dream but couldn't quite get there, so the floating happens instead. This is at least my current idea, will experiment more with this in the future and get back to you.

It's so nice to hear I'm not the only one who is having to deal with this though!! thanks for sharing!
It means some dignity and rank on the way.
What do you mean?
oh, well, i think i did an average job. also a lot of really experienced dream analysis people lurk on these forums, they just don't post much. thanks for the feedback.
Well I really wish one of them would give me some insight here

Old post but I had the same dream for a while but last night I got back up when I fell and someone even reacted to it but they got arrested immediately which was really creepy for some reason and then I started walking but here and there I could consciously make myself hop like an astronaut only floating too far when I started to lose focus. I wasn't in control though it just seemed like the dream played out exactly how I wanted but anyways I just thought your post was interesting because of the similarities though I never did reach above the clouds.
I have the same dreams recently. Do you have them still?
No they actually went away thank goodness! For me they were particularly distressing. How do they make you feel?
First 6-7 dreams I was able to control it, but last ones are terrible. I try to control it, I reach the ground after lots of efforts , then I start floating up again. I feel anxiety because of it :(

I can relate a lot to what both of you are saying. This happens to me too. It starts gradually when I'm walking, or standing still talking to someone. I usually try to hold on to something with me feet or hands but the longer it goes on for, the more difficult it gets to control it. It's really annoying/stressful and anxiety inducing. 

Sometimes, tho I have the type that I've seen some people mention where I'm out walking hy myself  where I use this to "power jump" to yet further in every stride, even leap over buildings at times. This can be somewhat enjoyable for a while, but when I try to change it up, to get back to walking normally, it instantly gets stressful and I can control it at all. I'm usually with someone when I don't like it at all, and am by myself when I can somewhat enjoy it and sometimes be semi-lucid and control it a little. 

I've had these dreams for as long as I can remember.  But more in periods.

Thanks for sharing guys!

I totally understand that. Personally, I never get back to the ground but I get being able to use the muscles that I think I need to use or not having the right muscles. All of a sudden I just take lift off and I’ll hit the top of the ceilings, and if I’m  outside, I keep going. however, most of the time I’m indoors the people I’m with don’t even notice that I’m floating. I just leave them behind and I float to the next thing that I see. I almost always dream that I am floating.

Mine starts after an argument then my left leg floats up as I lose balance my right starts to the point where I'm always touching the doorframe or ceiling. My panick sets in as the doors open and I'm eerily close to floating out, weirdly enough like all your stories and replies everyone interacts as normal. Sometimes opening the door while looking at me as if I wasn't floating.

You've literally described my ongoing nightmare, it's usually indoors for me too it's almost like heavy leg after the gym. Almost jelly like even if I get grounded again my legs skew into zero gravity. Almost spaghetti like without the cartoon style stretchy look. People just talk or talk over me as I ask for help. Your the only post I can find with eerily identical patterns. Hope your sleeping better and moving on, best wishes.
I've had this dream for years; I float up to the ceiling, desperately trying to hold myself down on the floor, life around me continues like nothing is happening.  Bizarre.

submission.title: Taoism and Nietzsche
submission.body:
Hello all. I am looking for help with a dilemma that has recently arisen for me, which is that I have come to thoroughly enjoy two separate philosophical systems.

On one hand, I feel I have experienced the benefits of adhering to the Tao, where non-action and stillness have bred significant positive results for me.

I have also, however, experienced great joy from Nietzsche's "will to power" and the invention of a new table of values for myself, as well as with the striving aspect of Nietzsche's philosophy which Taoism seems at odds with. 

Put simply, I am struggling to reconcile Nietzsche's encouraged deviance and "will to power" and the Taoist principles of wu-wei and humility. Is following the Tao inherently anti-Nietzsche?

Can anyone lend a helpful word? Even if no one has a straightforward answer, I would still love to hear your thoughts. I am getting some weird kind of emotional headache from this as if some cognitive dissonance is happening.

comments.body: Why not enjoy both?
In “Nietzsche and East Asian Thought,” Graham Parkes articulates a resonance between Taoism and Nietzsche.

To what is your disperency between the both? Maybe you have a belief that creates division between them that is not exactly apparant, a sort of stereotype between both Neitzche and Taosin that renders you a non-participant of the philosophies if you were to follow the other. I think they're compatible.

Yes, this is definitely the case. I am beginning, with the help of other kind redditors, to root it out. I am not sure if you are interested, but I think perhaps I made an ironic application of monotheistic ideas to Taoism. My misconception may have involved the Taoist idea of action through non-action. I think I was misconstruing this concept to emphasize doing nothing all the time, which I think is not what it means. I also think I made the mistake of making the Tao a kind of morality rather than the unknowable force it's meant to be. 

Should've re-read the first line of the Tao Te Ching :)
I think I was making thought-errors. My reply to another user here explains what I think the trouble is, I think I was doing a goofy perhaps, which caused conflict between the two systems.

Two key components of Nietzsches Superman is the mind of Ceaser with the heart of Christ. This is not inconsistent with what is taught in the Tao te Ching.      “The Tao loves and nourishes all things but does not lord it over them” 
One use for The Tao te Ching is the art of governance.

I suppose those philosophies exclude each other. The only religion Nietzsche praised was buddhism and even in that case he only enjoyed aspects of it that focused on prosperity of self and staying in the current of reincarnations ( first of all the “self” isn’t perceived in buddhism as real, secondly buddhists believe that the world is inherently flawed and in no such way that it can be fixed by action, thirdly the main focus of buddhism is to escape meaningless loop of reincarnations by entering nirvana (basically the state of not being) which is contradictory to nietzsche in regards of his - do not tarnish the only life you know for something unknown and supernatural). So even in religion that focuses on exiting a meaningless loop of reincarnations and becoming selfless nietzsche found a call for action and the praise for “self”. Imo those world views (Taoism and Nietzsche) are incompatible. If you were to try and merge them nietzsche would warp tao to the of point being unrecognizable.

P.s. sorry, i didn’t have to say much about tao. Hopefully analogy with buddhism is sufficient to make my point. Also just to clarify nietzsche chose a very specific tradition of buddhism (t’heravada) that focuses on self enlightenment (for which it was extensively criticized through out history). So he didn’t get everything wrong but surely did take a lot of things out of context to fit his narrative and philosophy.

I'd like to add this excellent paper by Graham Parkes, which focuses on what is IMO the most fruitful intersection:   
   
 
[The Wandering Dance: Chuang Tzu and Zarathustra](https://www.jstor.org/stable/1398826)

Taoism has several points of contact with some western philosophies which have influenced Nietzsche.

Stoicism certainly shares some aspects with Taoism, especially in the realm of ethics and cosmology. The Eleatic school also has several points of contact with Taoism, in particular the monistic nature of phenomena, which characterizes Spinoza too. Hegel and his absolute, the reconciling aspect of all seemingly opposites in nature (Hegel even taught classes on Taoism). Schopenhauer, Nietzsche's philosophical awakening, was deeply influenced by eastern philosophies such as Buddhism and Hinduism. His concept of the will is also a form of monism, which along with some form of determinism (which all of the schools above share up to a point) certainly has things in common with Taoism.

In the end it depends on your personality. Are you a more contemplative person? Or maybe you prefer action? Do you impose your will on the world or do you just get carried by the flow?

Think about who you are and get your answers. Also, you can share some viewpoints of a philosophy without having to share them all.
Read Heraclitus. He’s the link you’re looking for.

I think wu-wei can be completely in harmony with the will to power if you realize that Nietzsche thoroughly criticises the notion of free will. There are no free or unfree wills, only weak and strong wills, and if you seem to force yourself into something, it's a weak will trying to desperately push against a stronger one. Nietzsche is a fatalist, he literally advises you to not force yourself into anything, because you would be denying your inner nature, your "dominant instinct". That's why he puts such an emphasis on instincts and argues that actions done without much effort, thinking, and with ease ("on light feet") have much higher value, both ethical and aesthetical, than actions done with exerting a huge amount of "willpower" and struggle.
I also enjoy reading Zhuangzi and Nietzsche. No conflict there. They're doing two different things.

Yes! It's funny I read the DDJ before reading any Heraclitus and when I read Heraclitus, I ended up asking my professor if he or the people around him had any contact with China and could've been exposed to philosophy from there 😂

Will to Power is neither "will" nor "power", but "the instinct for freedom" (GM II, 18), as Nietzsche himself put it. Consider the distinction between *willingness*, on the one hand, and *willfulness*, on the other. Both are forms of will to power, but the first is affirmative, active, and creative, while the second is defiant, reactive, and destructive.

I enjoy both of them too. However, ‘will to power’ described by Nietzsche seems as projection of his own desires, we are all different, and I can understand his desire as much as I understand mine, which is absence of it. Wu wei of Tao is more about effortless being, which has different paths for different people. My wu wei is not searching for power, while there will be people who resonate with Nietzsche, and it is their natural desire. Nietzsche also supports the idea of one’s own values, as I remember, at least in Thus spoke Zarathushtra (it is better than to be a conformist/believer)

If you feel like you have desire to rule, it is kind of your natural being. As long as you don’t force yourself to seek power, it still resonates with your wu wei

Technically yes, we are bombarded by space rocks and dust all the time, but the amount of additional mass is comparatively so small the effects on gravity would be basically 0 over centuries or millennia or even millions of years.

>Does the mass of Earth change over time?

Yes. Some atmosphere is lost to the solar wind (very small amount) and dust and meteorites land on Earth (again, very small amount). These fluctuations are tiny, however, to the point of not being measurable.

>Does it change gravity or escape velocity?

In theory yes, in practice not really.  
The changes are so small that the even smaller influence they have on gravity is negligible.  
The gravitational waves from a neutron merger on the other side of the galaxy are more measurable than a change in Earth's mass to due to a meteorite strike.

The mass of the Earth changes as it acquires mass via micrometeoroids impinging on the atmosphere while atmospheric Hydrogen and Helium are slowly lost to space.  

The net annual change (-5 x 10^(7) kg) is entirely insignificant compared to the mass of the Earth (6 x 10^(24) kg)
Your post has been removed. For simple questions like these please use [the weekly "All space question" thread](https://www.reddit.com/r/space/about/sticky) pinned at the top of the subreddit.

submission.title: Answer to ‘why Finland is the happiest country in the world?’ ;)
submission.body: My Finnish friends are too shy to admit it, so I’ll do it for them! The secret to why Finland is the happiest country in the world? The bidet shower!! What could be more satisfying than washing your ass after a messy paska, with the cleanest water in the world?! ;)

comments.body: Handheld showers generally. Love traveling but it is always pain to find hotels with handheld showers.
Totally underestimated thing.
The pussy-asshole telephone

Ok, well Reddit removed my reply for including an illustration from the web, but no. You just spray water. Look it up, there are simple drawings of how to angle it. Don’t turn the water up too high either.
Ive only ever used it for cleaning the bathroom

*Soi pillupuhelin,*

*mä siihen vastasin*

*ja korvani kastelin.*
This right here is the most true thing said about Finland. Respect 💯
I use wipes and have a dedicated bin for disposal. Best I can do.
https://www.reddit.com/r/SipsTea/s/fvP97YYorm
I'm Brazil it is very common to have this at home too.
Wait that’s what I water my plants with. How do you get it to spray on your ass?
The third option is that they’re spraying the water in between the toilet rim and the seat. This causes a nice little waterfall op described :)

It’s the most “content” country. Not the “happiest”.

You put the bidet in between your legs from the front. Reach around the back with your other hand to help splash the water around and, once you have removed most of the excrement, apply a gentle rubbing motion on your anus to help the water do its work and achieve a squeaky clean finish. Once you're satisfied, put the bidet back and wash your hands in the sink, which in a well-designed bathroom should be reachable while still sitting on the toilet.

Lots of people seem to prefer using the bidet behind your back (and also not helping with their off-hand), but I've always found using it from the front much more ergonomic.

Here's a step-by-step guide in excruciating detail if the process still seems too complicated to understand:

1. Ensure ready access to toilet paper or towels (likely paper, but cotton towels aren't unheard of for home use -- a reusable cotton towel requires the expertise to know you have achieved the necessary level of cleanliness before you dry by wiping) for drying your hands and rear. Doing this before you start washing avoids the difficult situation of trying to get loo paper off the roll with wet hands, as well as the situation where you have to get up from the toilet with a wet bum to retrieve your absorbent medium of choice.
2. Inexperienced operators should consider flushing the toilet before beginning the washing process.
3. Turn on the tap and run your finger under it until it is body temperature. Err on the side of cooler rather than hotter -- hot water is more painful than cool water is unpleasant.
4. Retrieve the bidet shower. If the unit is unfamiliar, test its operation in the sink, or in the exceptional circumstance where a sink is not available, by carefully pointing it into the toilet water between your legs.
5. Using whichever hand you don't normally use for wiping, insert the bidet shower between your legs, showerhead facing up. The tip of the shower should be pointing downwards at an angle, and should not be touching the water in the bowl. Be careful not to hit the trigger prematurely. You will be pushing the trigger with your thumb.
6. With the hand you're accustomed to wiping with, reach around your back as if you were using toilet paper. Steel yourself in the face of the fact that you are about to touch your own anus after you just finished excreting. Take solace in the fact that you are doing so while spraying it and your hand with clean water, and will be washing your hands afterwards.
6. Before your wiping hand reaches its final destination, gradually press down the trigger on the bidet shower. A gradual trigger push ensures minimal collateral damage and allows aim adjustment should the showerhead be misaligned.
7. Once the water stream is stable and aimed where it should be, use the cupped fingers of your off-hand to lap up the water from the stream back up towards your posterior. The varying wave-like motion of the water thus created removes soiling much more efficiently than the direct water stream alone would.
8. Once the majority of the detritus has been removed, touch the fingers of your off-hand to your rear while continuously spraying water and apply a gentle rubbing motion.
9. Once the tactile sensory feedback from your fingertips indicates that you have achieved the desired level of cleanliness, stop the water stream. Allow your off-hand a brief moment to drip off most of the water wetting it into the toilet bowl.
10. Replace the bidet shower in its holder.
11. Wash your hands in the sink. Soap can be used if it is available (and if it is available, you can also use it on your bum in the preceding steps if your bowel movement was especially troublesome), but it is also permissible to omit using it at this stage, because you will be washing your hands a second time once you're no longer sitting on the toilet.
  - A variation of this step is to use a length of toilet paper to dry and clean off your off-hand. This may be necessary in bathrooms where the sink is not within easy reach of the toilet seat.
12. Take a length of toilet paper (about 4 sheets, depending on how thick the paper is and how ecological you are feeling) and gently pat your posterior until it is dry. Assuming the previous steps were correctly carried out, there is no need to wipe at this point -- wiping can abrade the skin and contribute to hemorrhoids.
13. Get up from the toilet, pull up your pants, close the toilet lid and flush the toilet.
14. Wash your hands with soap.

submission.title: How to get to Denia from airport in Alicante after 10 pm?
submission.body: Is it still possible to find a bus/tram?

comments.body: Normal buses and TRAM stop at around 22:30 the latest. You could get the 23:00 bus from the airport to Benidorm but then you would have to either wait until morning or take a quite expensive taxi trip. If you're arriving past 23:00 the only bus running would be the one from the airport to Alicante city, same deal as Benidorm.
Taxi
Is there only cabify ?
Your best option is a taxi. There is a rank which can get busy, or you can book in advance with one of the options available (eg Beniconnect for private and shared transfers)
Cabify, uber, local taxi....
